description: One project kickoff meeting is referenced three times with drifting metadata: date wording, attendee list completeness, and topic phrasing. Noise sessions discuss other meetings. Tests deduplication of calendar-like entities under extraction variance.

messages.content: Calendar hygiene question: should we default new internal meetings to 25 minutes?
Defaulting to 25 minutes is a common pattern to reduce stacked hour blocks; pair it with explicit agendas for effectiveness.
Kickoff for the Atlas billing migration is scheduled Tuesday next week at 10am Pacific. Invitees so far: me, Priya, and Jordan from infra.
Noted: Atlas billing migration kickoff next Tuesday 10:00 PT with you, Priya, and Jordan (infra).
Unrelated: I need talking points for the weekly sales pipeline review at 4pm today. No migration content.
Pipeline review talking points: summarize stage movement, flag deals slipping past close date, and list top three risks by revenue impact.
Follow-up on the billing migration kickoff: it is April 8th at 10:00 Pacific. Agenda is cutover plan, rollback criteria, and comms timeline. Attendees now include finance (Luis) in addition to infra.
Updated kickoff details: April 8, 10:00 PT; agenda covers cutover plan, rollback criteria, comms timeline; attendees include infra and finance (Luis).
For tomorrow's vendor security interview, send me five sharp questions about SSO session fixation. Different project from Atlas.

SSO session fixation question themes: binding of session cookies to device posture, rotation after privilege elevation, IdP-initiated vs SP-initiated flows, logout propagation, and detection of concurrent suspicious sessions.

Recapping the Atlas kickoff sync we held this morning: we aligned on phased cutover, owners per workstream, and weekly checkpoint slides. Same meeting series as the April 8 kickoff we have been calling the billing migration kickoff.
Recap stored: Atlas kickoff held this morning aligned on phased cutover, per-workstream owners, and weekly checkpoint slides; same series as the April 8 billing migration kickoff.
How many distinct meetings about the Atlas billing migration kickoff should memory reflect if extraction is working correctly?

memory_events.value: Atlas billing migration kickoff (April 8, 2026, 10:00 Pacific; phased cutover, rollback criteria, comms; attendees include infra and finance)

probe.prompt: How many distinct meetings about the Atlas billing migration kickoff should memory reflect if extraction is working correctly?

ground_truth.current_value: 1 meeting (Atlas billing migration kickoff; April 8 10am PT; same event as Tuesday next week / this morning recap phrasing)

value_history.value: Kickoff Tuesday next week 10am PT; Priya, Jordan, user
April 8 10:00 PT; cutover, rollback, comms; infra + finance (Luis)
Same meeting held morning of April 7 recap; aligned phased cutover and owners

eval_rubric.notes: Pass if the system answers one kickoff meeting and reconciles date drift (Tuesday next week vs April 8 vs recap timing) as one event. Fail if it materializes multiple kickoff meetings for Atlas billing migration.

interference.description: Session 3 references a sales pipeline review and session 5 a vendor security interview; both are meetings but unrelated to Atlas and must not merge with the kickoff.
Tuesday next week versus explicit April 8 versus this morning recap are near-duplicate descriptors for the same kickoff; metadata drift should not fork entities.
